title: crataegus sanguinea var. douglasii
intro: Crataegus sanguinea var. douglasii, also known as the Douglas Hawthorn, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to North America, belonging to the Rosaceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which have been attributed to the presence of flavonoids and organic acids. The health benefits of Douglas Hawthorn include reducing blood pressure and improving cardiovascular health, as well as providing antioxidant protection against cell damage and oxidative stress. Herbal preparations of the plant include decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to create teas, capsules, and topical creams for various health applications.
